MicroCosmos pairs scanning electron and brightfield microscopy with patient
            fieldwork. Each specimen is sampled from rivers, mosses, soils, and ocean foam,
            then imaged, annotated, and archived alongside its environmental context — so
            anyone can study the life that surrounds us at scales we rarely consider. A field guide to the world you can't see. diatom microscope photograph

MicroCosmos is a research-grade visual atlas of microscopic life — diatoms,
            ciliates, spores, and crystalline waterborne worlds, captured at 400× and beyond. The universe lives inside a single drop. microscopic cell organisms vivid blue
